root.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Issue Management Guide
root.summary: Front matter and introduction for the Cúram 8.1.2 Issue Management Guide. It identifies the guide’s purpose and audience, explains the case-based issue management model, and outlines the full lifecycle: configuring issue types, ownership, resolutions, and approval checks; creating, assigning, resolving, approving, and closing issues; tracking statuses, history, workflow events, and appeals. It also covers supporting features such as My Issues, attachments, communications, notes, tasks, and user roles. The section includes the table of contents, edition applicability for Cúram 8.1–8.1.2, and legal material: copyright, usage permissions, warranties/disclaimers, export and patent notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

children.title: Note
children.summary: This note directs readers to review the Notices section on page 19 before using the product information. It also states the copyright notice for Merative US L.P., covering 2012 and 2024.

children.title: Edition
Edition — pages 5-14
children.summary: Cúram Issue Management Guide edition notes for Cúram 8.1, 8.1.1, and 8.1.2. This section introduces the guide’s scope and audience, then outlines issue management concepts: issue administration setup, issue types, ownership, resolutions, and approval checks; case-level issue creation, assignment, resolution entry, approval, closure, statuses, and resolution history; plus additional features like my issues, attachments, communications, notes, tasks, and roles. Useful as the entry point for understanding how issues are configured and processed end to end.

children.title: Edition — pages 15-18
children.summary: Covers issue management features in Cúram: resolution approval workflow, automatic task creation for supervisor approval, approval/rejection outcomes, and issue-related events such as opened, submitted, approved, rejected, and closed. Also describes additional issue features including My Issues, attachments, communications, notes with history and security, tasks, and user roles (case owner and supervisor). Concludes with a high-level recap of issue lifecycle and links to related guides.

Introductory section for the Cúram Issue Management Guide, applicable to Cúram 8.1, 8.1.1, and 8.1.2. It defines the guide’s purpose, audience, and case-based issue management model, then outlines the scope: issue administration (issue types, ownership, resolutions, approval checks), issue processing (create, assign, resolve, approve, close, statuses, history, workflow events), and additional features (My Issues, attachments, communications, notes, tasks, roles). Useful as the entry point to the full issue lifecycle and related configuration concepts.

children.title: Notices
Privacy policy
children.summary: This page covers legal and attribution information: fictitious sample data notice, licensing terms for sample programs, “as is” warranty disclaimer and liability limits, the Merative privacy policy link, and a list of trademarks (Merative, IBM, Adobe, Oracle, Linux, Microsoft, UNIX). It is mainly useful for copyright, usage, and trademark references rather than product functionality.

children.title: Trademarks
children.summary: Lists trademark notices and legal attribution for Merative and third-party brands. Covers Merative, IBM, Adobe, Oracle/Java, Linux, Microsoft, and UNIX trademarks, plus a note that other company/product names may belong to others. Includes privacy policy link and copyright/license disclaimers stating sample programs are provided “as is” without warranty.

Legal notices and usage permissions for the publication: personal and enterprise reuse is allowed only if proprietary notices are preserved, but redistribution, derivative works, and external sharing require Merative consent. It disclaims warranties, limits liability, notes possible patent rights, and states export-law compliance is required. It also warns that third-party product and website information is unverified and non-endorsement. Includes copyright © Merative US L.P. 2012, 2024.

children.title: Contents
children.summary: Table of contents for the Cúram Issue Management Guide. Lists front matter plus chapter 1 sections on issue administration, issue management, and additional features, with page references for topics such as issue type configuration, ownership, resolution approval, creating and closing issues, statuses, workflow, attachments, communications, notes, tasks, and user roles. Also includes additional information, notices, privacy policy, and trademarks.

children.title: 1 Cúram Issue Management Guide
1.1 Introduction
Purpose
children.summary: This section explains the purpose of the Cúram Issue Management Guide and the issue management functionality it covers. It states that issues can be created on cases to handle discrepancies in client evidence during eligibility determination, with snapshots of evidence preserved at creation time. It also highlights that issues can be resolved through workflows and appealed, and that the guide is intended for business analysts familiar with Social Enterprise Management and case management.

children.title: Audience
children.summary: Intended for business analysts in the organization, this section introduces Cúram Issue Management and explains that it helps manage discrepancies in client evidence during case processing. It covers the guide’s purpose, required familiarity with Social Enterprise Management and case management, and the core capabilities: creating issues manually or automatically, viewing evidence snapshots and current evidence, tracking issue progress, and prioritizing issues.

children.title: Prerequisites
children.summary: Assumes the reader knows basic Social Enterprise Management and is familiar with case management processes in the application. This prerequisite section supports the issue management guide, which explains how issues are created on cases to resolve evidence discrepancies, track and prioritize issues, view evidence snapshots, and manage outcomes through workflows and appeals.

children.title: Background
children.summary: Introduces issue management in Cúram. It explains that discrepancies in client evidence versus third-party verification may require investigation during case processing, such as conflicting reasons for leaving a job. The application supports creating issues automatically or manually, viewing both the evidence snapshot at issue creation and current evidence, tracking issue progress, and prioritizing issues. It also notes that unresolved issues may affect case handling, including possible suspension of payments.

children.title: Chapters in this Guide
children.summary: This section outlines the guide’s structure and introduces the main issue-management topics. It lists three chapters: Issue Administration, Issue Management, and Additional Features. It then begins Issue Administration, explaining that issue data is configured in system administration and inherited by case-level issues. Covered topics include issue type configuration, resolution administration, and resolution approval checks, including effective date settings that control when issues can be created.

Introduces the Cúram Issue Management Guide and its purpose: explaining issue management functionality used to resolve evidence discrepancies during eligibility determination. It states the audience is business analysts familiar with Social Enterprise Management and case management. The section describes situations where client evidence and third-party verification conflict, requiring investigation and case-level issue handling. Core capabilities include creating issues manually or automatically, preserving an evidence snapshot at creation time, viewing current evidence, tracking issue progress, and prioritizing issues.

children.title: 1.2 Issue Administration
Introduction
children.summary: Introduction to issue administration in Cúram 8.1.2. Explains that issues can be created on cases only after system administration configures issue information, which is then inherited by case-level issues. Covers the main setup areas: issue type configuration, resolution administration, and issue resolution approval checks. Describes issue types as templates with unique names and effective date ranges; an issue cannot be created if its configured period has not started or has ended.

children.title: Issue Type Configuration
children.summary: Issue types are system administration templates for case-level issues. Each issue type has unique generic information, typically named for the related evidence type (for example, earned income). Administrators also configure effective dating with start and end dates, which determine when issues of that type can be created on cases. If the effective period has not started or has ended, the issue cannot be created.

children.title: Configuring Issue Ownership
children.summary: This section explains configuring issue ownership for an issue type. It describes an Issue Ownership Strategy that uses workflow to determine the initial owner of a new issue, similar to case ownership. Ownership can be overridden to assign issues to a user, organization unit, position, or work queue. It also covers related administration topics: issue priorities, issue home pages, workflow events on issue creation, resolution setup, and resolution approval checks at user, organization unit, and issue configuration levels.

children.title: Resolution Administration
children.summary: This section explains how issue resolutions are administered in Cúram Issue Management. It covers configuring selectable resolution codes, optionally attaching workflow events to trigger case processing (for example, unsuspending payment), and defining resolution approval checks. Approval checks can be set at the user, organization unit, or issue type/configuration level, with the system evaluating them in that order. It is useful for understanding how resolutions are created, approved, and linked to workflow behavior.

children.title: Issue Resolution Approval Checks
children.summary: Explains how issue resolution approval checks determine whether a resolution is manually or automatically approved. Checks can be configured at the user, organization unit, or issue type/configuration level, with user-level overriding org unit, and org unit overriding issue configuration. User and org unit checks can apply to a single issue or all issues. When a resolution is submitted, the system evaluates these levels in order to decide approval behavior.

This section overviews issue administration setup in Cúram 8.1.2. It explains that issues can be created on cases only after system administration configures issue information, which is inherited by case issues. Key topics are issue type configuration (templates with unique names, effective dates, priority, home page, and workflow events), issue ownership strategy, resolution administration (selectable resolution codes and workflow-triggered case actions), and resolution approval checks at user, organization unit, and issue type levels.

children.title: 1.3 Issue Management
Introduction
children.summary: Introduces issue management at the case level in Cúram 8.1.2. Explains that issues are case-specific instances of issue types, can be created manually or automatically during case processing when evidence discrepancies are found, and may also track caseworker questions/problems. Covers manual creation details: linking evidence type/type, storing an immutable evidence snapshot, recording start/end dates and priority. Also describes issue ownership: initial owner defaults to the case owner, ownership can transfer, and can be assigned to users or organization groups.

children.title: Creating an Issue
Automatic Creation
children.summary: Explains how issues are created at the case level in Cúram issue management. Focuses on automatic issue creation during case processing when discrepancies are detected between pieces of evidence, based on organization-defined circumstances. Also distinguishes this from manual creation, noting evidence snapshots, linked current evidence, dates, priority, and ownership assignment. Useful for understanding when the system can create issues without user action.

children.title: Manual Creation
children.summary: Manual issue creation lets a user create a case-level issue linked to a specific evidence type and issue type. The user identifies the evidence that triggered the issue; the system stores an immutable snapshot of that evidence and links it to the current evidence. The user also sets the issue start/end dates and priority, which defaults from the issue type but can be changed. On creation, the case owner is assigned as initial issue owner, with ownership transferable to a user or organization group.

Explains case-level issue creation in Cúram issue management. Issues can be created automatically during case processing when rules detect evidence discrepancies, based on organization-defined circumstances, or manually by a user. Manual creation requires selecting the related evidence type and issue type, capturing an immutable evidence snapshot linked to current evidence, and setting start/end dates and priority. On creation, the case owner becomes the initial issue owner, and ownership can later be transferred to a user or organization group.

children.title: Assigning Issue Ownership
children.summary: This section explains how issue ownership is assigned and managed. When an issue is created, the system initially assigns ownership to the case owner of the case where the issue was raised. Ownership can later be transferred. An issue owner may be an individual user or an organization group such as an organization unit, position, or work queue, allowing any member to work the issue. It also notes that the default ownership strategy is configurable by the agency.

children.title: Entering an Issue Resolution
children.summary: Explains how issues are resolved and closed in Cúram Issue Management. A caseworker enters a resolution after reviewing evidence; resolutions come from issue-type configuration and can trigger workflow events. Resolutions must usually be submitted for supervisor approval, with statuses submitted/approved; approved resolutions automatically close the issue, while rejected ones must be resubmitted. Issues can also be manually closed with a reason, and the case owner is notified if someone else closes it. Includes the issue statuses: open, closed, and canceled.

children.title: Approving an Issue Resolution
children.summary: Explains how an issue resolution is entered, approved, and how this affects issue closure. A caseworker records a resolution based on adjudicated evidence, using resolutions configured for the issue type and triggering any associated workflow events. The resolution is then submitted for supervisor approval; it may be auto-approved if the submitter has approval rights or is the supervisor. Submitted vs. approved statuses are defined, rejected resolutions must be resubmitted, and approved resolutions automatically close the issue. Also notes manual closure, closure notifications, editable closure details, and issue statuses (open, closed, canceled).

children.title: Closing an Issue
children.summary: This section explains how an issue is closed in Issue Management. It covers automatic closure when an issue resolution is approved, and manual closure at any stage when the underlying evidence has been corrected. It notes that manual closure requires a reason, may notify the case owner if another user closes it, and that closure details can later be changed by a system user. It also introduces issue statuses: open, closed, and canceled.

children.title: Issue Statuses
children.summary: Explains issue lifecycle and status handling in Cúram Issue Management. Issues start open and can move to closed or canceled. A resolution must be entered before an issue can be resolved; resolutions come from issue-type configuration and may trigger workflow events. Resolutions require approval by a supervisor unless the submitter has approval rights, with statuses submitted and approved; rejected resolutions must be resubmitted. Approved resolutions close the issue automatically, though issues can also be manually closed with a reason and owner notification.

children.title: Issue Resolution Statuses
children.summary: Explains issue and resolution processing statuses in Cúram 8.1.2. Issues can be Open, Closed (manually or automatically after resolution approval), or Canceled. Resolutions have four statuses: In-edit, Submitted, Approved, and Rejected. Also covers automatic resolution status history maintenance, including status changes and effective dates, and notes that submitted resolutions are recorded in the resolution approval requests list.

children.title: Resolution Status History
children.summary: Explains issue and resolution lifecycle statuses in Cúram 8.1.2. Issues can be Open, Closed, or Canceled; resolutions can be In-edit, Submitted, Approved, or Rejected. The section focuses on resolution status history: it is automatically maintained for all issue resolutions, updated when a resolution is entered, submitted, approved, or rejected, and records the resolution, status, and effective date to track progress over time.

children.title: Viewing the List of Resolution Approval Requests
children.summary: Explains resolution approval workflow statuses and history, leading into the approval-request list. Covers issue statuses (open, closed, canceled), resolution statuses (in-edit, submitted, approved, rejected), and how resolution status history records each change with effective dates. Introduces that when a caseworker submits a resolution for approval, a resolution approval request is automatically added to a list of requests. Useful for understanding where approval requests come from and how resolution progress is tracked.

children.title: Issue Resolution Workflow
children.summary: This section explains the issue resolution approval workflow. When a case worker submits a resolution, the system creates a task for the case supervisor to approve it, and notifies the case worker of the outcome. It also describes the issue events calendar, where the system automatically records key events such as Issue Opened, Resolution Submitted, Resolution Approved, Resolution Rejected, and Issue Closed. It briefly introduces additional issue features, including the automatically maintained My Issues list.

children.title: Events
children.summary: Describes the issue events calendar and the system-generated events tracked for each issue. Each calendar shows event name and date, and events are automatically created during issue processing: Issue Opened, Resolution Submitted, Resolution Approved, Resolution Rejected, and Issue Closed. Also notes that these events appear on the issue calendar and are recorded at the corresponding lifecycle points.

Cúram issue management at the case level covers creating and managing issues, including automatic creation from evidence discrepancies and manual creation with evidence type, issue type, immutable evidence snapshot, dates, and priority. It explains issue ownership defaults to the case owner and can be transferred to users or organization groups. It also covers resolution entry, approval/rejection workflow, automatic or manual closure, closure notifications, issue and resolution statuses, and resolution status history plus approval-request tracking.

children.title: 1.4 Additional Features
Introduction
children.summary: This introduction outlines issue resolution workflow and issue event tracking in Cúram Issue Management. It explains how submitted resolutions are automatically routed for case supervisor approval, how approval/rejection is recorded, and that notifications go back to the case worker. It also describes the issue events calendar and the system-generated events for issue lifecycle milestones (opened, resolution submitted/approved/rejected, closed). Finally, it notes additional issue features, including a maintained “My Issues” list for viewing all issues on a case.

children.title: My Issues
children.summary: “My Issues” describes the issues list feature in Cúram Issue Management. The system automatically maintains a list of all issues on a case, and the issues list page lets users view them. The surrounding text also covers issue resolution approval workflow, supervisor approval tasks, notifications to case workers, and system-generated issue events such as Issue Opened, Resolution Submitted/Approved/Rejected, and Issue Closed.

children.title: Attachments
children.summary: Attachments are supplemental files attached to an issue, such as scanned documents, Word, Excel, or PDF files, and can be accessed by users with appropriate security privileges. This section also briefly covers related issue information types: communications (paper, phone, or email correspondence tied to an issue), notes (free-text entries with priority, sensitivity, edit and history tracking), tasks, and user roles (case owner and case supervisor responsibilities).

children.title: Communications
children.summary: Communications are issue-related correspondence to or from the organization, automatically linked to the issue when created. They may be paper, telephone, or email based, and can integrate with Microsoft Word templates, XSL templates, or email servers. A communication can also be recorded for the case member whose evidence caused the issue. This section sits alongside related issue content such as attachments, notes, tasks, and user roles.

children.title: Notes
children.summary: Notes let users add free-text information to an issue, prioritize it, and assign a sensitivity rating to restrict access. Users can modify a note’s priority and sensitivity, append more text, and, if configured, edit the original text for a limited time. The system keeps a note history with each version, timestamps, and the user who made changes.

children.title: Tasks
children.summary: This section introduces issue-related tasks and user roles. It explains that a case owner is the user responsible for an issue, while a case supervisor oversees its management. It follows related content on attachments, communications, and notes, which describe supporting issue information such as uploaded files, correspondence, and editable notes with history and sensitivity controls.

children.title: User Roles
children.summary: Defines issue user roles in Cúram: the case owner is the user responsible for an issue, and the case supervisor oversees management of the issue. This section is brief and mainly clarifies the two role types used to assign accountability within an issue.

This section introduces additional issue features in Cúram Issue Management. It notes that issues include a system-maintained “My Issues” list for viewing all issues on a case, building on the issue resolution workflow where submitted resolutions are routed to the case supervisor for approval and case workers are notified of the outcome. It also refers to issue event tracking via an events calendar with automatic milestones such as Issue Opened, Resolution Submitted, Approved, Rejected, and Issue Closed.

children.title: 1.5 Conclusion
Summary
children.summary: This section summarizes key issue-related concepts in Cúram 8.1.2: attachments (supported file types and access by authorized users), communications (paper, phone, email, and template/email integration), notes (free text, priority, sensitivity, editable text, and history), tasks, and user roles for issues (case owner and supervisor responsibilities). It serves as a concluding recap of the guide’s main topics.

children.title: Additional Information
children.summary: This section supplements the Cúram Issue Management Guide with references to related core guides for case processing, participant, nominee, financial, communications, workflow, service planning, verification, and evidence concepts. It then provides legal notices covering permitted personal and commercial use, restrictions on distribution and derivative works, export compliance, warranty disclaimers, patent and third-party product statements, sample code licensing, privacy policy link, and trademark listings.

This conclusion recaps the guide’s core issue-management concepts: attachments, communications, notes, tasks, and user roles. It summarizes that issues are created from case eligibility, progress through resolution approval and closure, and can be assigned to case owners and supervisors. The section also points to related Cúram core guides for case processing, participants, nominees, financials, communications, workflow, service planning, verification, and evidence. It then includes legal notices on usage rights, warranties, export compliance, sample code, privacy, and trademarks.

Overview of Cúram Issue Management. Explains why issues are raised during eligibility determination when client evidence conflicts with third-party verification, and what the guide covers for business analysts. Includes system setup of issue types, ownership, resolutions, and approval checks; case-level issue creation (manual or automatic), immutable evidence snapshots, ownership transfer, resolution workflow, approval/rejection, status history, and event tracking. Also covers add-ons like My Issues, attachments, communications, notes, tasks, user roles, plus conclusion and legal notices.
